name: technology_staffing_model_assigns_overtime_lumen_lib_eq
premise: A zero-trust rollout locked out on-call engineers from production; break-glass credentials expired yesterday. Telemetry and witness accounts diverge; choosing one narrative for speed will discard evidence that may matter in court next month. Key facts are still arriving from the field; the team is working off stale dashboards and verbal handoffs. An automated batch or failover job is scheduled at the top of the hour; after it runs, rollback cost jumps by an order of magnitude. No option cleanly satisfies both liberty and equality; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Keisha Diallo

agents.name: Elena Berg

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so equality does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so liberty does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Keisha Diallo must choose under liberty versus equality. What does Keisha Diallo do?
